description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-
rationale:
The <tt>/etc/gshadow-</tt> file is a backup of <tt>/etc/gshadow</tt>, and as such,
it contains group password hashes. Protection of this file is critical for system security.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/gshadow-
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: ----------
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/gshadow-" file to "0000" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/gshadow- file must have mode 0000 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/gshadow- file must have mode 0000 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.
stig.vuldiscussion: The "/etc/gshadow-" file is a backup of "/etc/gshadow", and as such, contains group password hashes. Protection of this file is critical for system security.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the "/etc/gshadow-" file has mode "0000" with the following command:

$ sudo stat -c "%a %n" /etc/gshadow-

0 /etc/gshadow-

If a value of "0" is not returned, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Change the mode of the file "/etc/gshadow-" to "0000" by running the following command:

$ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
